title: Gamma_0(23) Quantum Graph
description: The Hecke congruence subgroup Gamma_0(23) defines a quantum graph with 2 cusps connected by 15 bonds (primes <= 47). Bond lengths are c * log(p).

secular_eigenvalues.description: Eigenvalues of the secular equation det(S(k) - I) = 0 for the quantum graph scattering matrix

zeta_zero_comparison.note: The high KS distance (0.753) at this scale indicates the quantum graph spectrum does not directly reproduce individual zeta zeros but shares statistical universality class.

berkolaiko_winn_conditions.description: Four conditions for GUE spectral statistics on quantum graphs (Berkolaiko-Winn 2010)

conditions.name: Time-reversal
conditions.detail: Symmetric (GOE), not broken (GUE requires broken T-symmetry)

berkolaiko_winn_conditions.note: BW score 2/4 means the Gamma_0(23) graph alone does not produce GUE statistics. The coupling matrix J and the full operator H_D are needed to break time-reversal symmetry and achieve GUE.

level_sweep.description: Gamma_0(N) for N=1..50, showing why N=23 is selected
level_sweep.selection_criterion: psi(p) = p + 1 = 24 = Omega for p = 23 (supersingular prime)
level_sweep.note: Gamma_0(N) for N=1..50 can be computed from psi(N) = N * prod(1 + 1/p) over p|N
